summary: Blazing Jerry, tight set and soaring Weather Report Suite
blurb: The show shines with a smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and an epic Eyes Of The World into Weather Report Suite. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Keith’s organ adds perfect texture to the suite. The second set builds to a soaring climax with Let It Grow and Sugar Magnolia.
review:
On November 25, 1973 at Tempe’s Feyline Field the Dead delivered one of those desert‑night marvels that still feels fresh. From the opening “The Promised Land” they warmed up nicely, but the real magic ignited when the band launched into a smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, with Jerry’s guitar blazing and Bob’s spirited vocals riding the wave. The first set capped off with a powerful Playing In The Band that featured Phil’s devastating feedback climax.

The second set was a masterclass in flow: Around & Around rolled straight into an inspired Eyes Of The World that seamlessly segued into the Weather Report Suite Prelude and Part 1, allowing Keith’s organ to swirl around Jerry’s soaring leads. Let It Grow lifted the energy even higher before the band closed with Sugar Magnolia, Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad, One More Saturday Night and a warm “And We Bid You Good Night.” Across three pristine soundboard sources the performance is consistently praised as tight, energetic, and full of moments that still sparkle for Deadheads today.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly highlighting Jerry’s on‑fire guitar, tight set flow, and standout sequences (China>Rider, Eyes Of The World > Weather Report Suite). No negative commentary appears, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Near‑perfect soundboard transfer praised for excellent clarity, vocal presence and minimal tape noise; captures the full energy of the night.

key_highlights: Smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Eyes Of The World into Weather Report Suite (Prelude + Part 1) leading to Let It Grow
Jerry’s guitar on fire throughout, especially during Eyes Of The World and the suite
Crystal‑clear SBD capture that preserves every nuance of the performance
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar is described as "on fire" all night, delivering creative wah‑wah tones and driving solos on China>Rider, Eyes Of The World and throughout the set; vocals are in great voice.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a powerful bass foundation with superb work on Eyes Of The World and a devastating feedback climax at the end of Playing In The Band.
band_performance.Bob: Spirited vocal delivery, iconic "dig the sunset" comment, and leads We Bid You Good Night alongside Donna Jean; occasional early‑set over‑zealousness noted but overall praised.
band_performance.Keith: Organ tone is perfect and integral to the textures of Weather Report Suite and the China>Rider sequence, adding depth and color throughout.
band_performance.Billy: Sets tempo and propels the groove, especially during high‑energy sections; credited for driving the momentum in the second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
And We Bid You Good Night
